other_observers: Brandon Percival, Marggie Joy, Pearl Sandstrom-Smith, Cliff Smith, Bryan Patrick; Brandon Percival found this bird in the A.M.
duration: on and off for two hours
specific_location: west end of Pueblo Reservoir in the state wildlife area.
description: The was a medium sized gull, slightly smaller than nearby Ring-billed Gulls. The mantle color was a few shades darker than the RBGUs. The all yellow bill was short and at times "disappered" as there was no dark tip to contrast with the water coloration. The head was pale with a dark eye and a smudge below the eye. The most noticeable feature was the remains of the dark gray or blackish head that formed an arc at the rear of the bird's head extending dowwards to the auricular.Behaviors: Mostly loafing but occasionally got up for 2-5 seconds and then landed again.Call: none heardPlumage: adult basic
photographer: Photographer: bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard
